news.title: Founders Fund launches game show starring Sam Altman, Palmer Luckey, and other tech elites
news.summary: The debut episode, moderated by Founders Fund chief marketing officer Mike Solana, included a star-studded cast of current tech luminaries.

news.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
news.summary: Anthropic has been growing at a breakneck pace. The company announced that annualized revenue crossed $47 billion in May, up dramatically from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. That trajectory faces a real test, though.

news.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
news.summary: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

news.title: Filtr is a new privacy tool that blocks ads in almost every iPhone and Mac app
news.summary: This popular ad blocker app for iPhones, iPads, and Macs can now block ads from loading inside apps, including web browsers, thanks to a new feature in the latest Apple software.

news.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
news.summary: On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversation exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking place across venture capital, defense technology, artificial intelligence, and advanced industry. Secure

news.title: Valve says it’s ready to launch the Steam Machine this summer
news.summary: Valve now says that the delayed Steam Machine PC and Steam Frame VR headset are set to launch sometime this summer. In a Thursday blog post detailing its Verified programs for both pieces of hardware, Valve concludes by saying that "We're excited for players to try your titles on the new Steam hardw

news.title: Cyberdecks used to look like little laptops, but now they’re getting more personal
news.summary: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The Cut and Wired, went viral earlier this year with a mermaid-themed cyberdeck she made inside an old purse.

news.title: Kevin O’Leary agrees to downsize massive Utah data center
news.summary: Kevin O'Leary agreed to halve the size of his planned 40,000-acre data center in Utah amid mounting pressure from residents and activists, as reported earlier by local affiliate ABC4. news.title: Google is letting social media stars customize their search result page
news.summary: Google now lets big creators and publishers in the US claim dedicated profiles in Search to highlight things like videos, articles, and their other profiles online. But this feature won't be available to most people or organizations; Google is limiting it to people with at least 100,000 YouTube subs

news.title: Amazon’s new plan for games: James Bond and AI Snoop Dogg
news.summary: Amazon's gaming strategy has never really been clear. news.title: The skeptic’s guide to humanoid robots going viral on the Internet
news.summary: Robot demonstrations can distort public perceptions of robotic capabilities.

news.title: AT&T and Verizon lose Supreme Court case over fines for selling location data
news.summary: FCC did not violate carriers' right to jury trial, court says in 8-1 ruling.

news.title: These LLMs are the best at resisting Russian propaganda
news.summary: Estonian government benchmark shows how dozens of models combat Russia's "strategic narratives."

news.title: Dashlane explains how attackers managed to download encrypted password vaults
news.summary: By targeting large numbers of users, attackers increased their chances of success.

news.title: Elon Musk tries again to escape FTC audits of X data handling
news.summary: Musk can't be trusted to protect X user privacy, public commenters warn FTC.

news.title: Not to Alarm Anyone, but Flesh-Eating Screwworms Have Entered the US
news.summary: The USDA this week confirmed the first known infection of the carnivorous fly larva, which feast on the flesh of living mammals, after the United States eradicated the nightmare bugs in the 1960s.

news.title: The AI IPO Race Heats Up, DOGE Whistleblower Sues Elon Musk, and Instagram Gets Hacked
news.summary: On Uncanny Valley, we dive into the IPO bonanza that the top AI companies are embarking on to the point where some real estate listings are looking for not just regular old cash, but Anthropic stock.

news.title: Phoebe Bridgers Ditched the Internet to Hype Up Her New Music. It’s Working
news.summary: The indie artist has played a string of surprise, small-venue shows with no phones allowed, prompting fans to piece together clues about a potential upcoming album.

news.title: Meta Silently Added Face-Recognition Code for Its Smart Glasses to Millions of Phones
news.summary: Code reviewed by WIRED uncovered an unreleased face-recognition system embedded in Meta’s smart glasses platform. It’s designed to identify people via biometric data stored on users’ phones.

news.title: The TikTok Ban Was Never About TikTok
news.summary: A new documentary chronicles how the app became a stand-in for American anxieties about social media, China, and political power.
